title: Indulgent Spicy Polish Wrap
description: A spicy, springy, and indulgent Polish dish wrapped in a corn husk wrapper. Serve with your favorite sides for a filling meal.

ingredients.name: ground pork
ingredients.notes: 80/20 lean to fat ratio for juiciness

instructions: Blanch 4 corn husks in boiling water for 2 minutes to make pliable.
In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat. Add 1 minced onion and 2 cloves of minced garlic and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add 1 teaspoon of caraway seeds and 1 teaspoon of ground cumin to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add 1 pound of ground pork to the skillet, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks, until it's no longer pink.
Add 1 cup of chopped cabbage and 1 cup of chopped bell pepper to the skillet and cook until the vegetables are slightly tender.
Season the mixture with salt and pepper to taste.
To assemble the wraps, lay a corn husk flat on a work surface. Spoon 1/2 cup of the pork and vegetable mixture onto the center of the corn husk, leaving a 1-inch border on either side.
Fold the sides of the corn husk over the filling, then fold the top down to create a compact bundle.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients to make 4-6 wraps, depending on serving size.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
